footer.dataAttribution: Data: EIPA / UDT Poland (charging infrastructure), OpenEV Data (electric vehicles).

home.title: EVSource
home.description: Normalized data on charging stations and EV models in Poland — full coverage across all 16 provinces, ready for benchmarking by charging network operators.
home.setupMessage: Database is not configured yet. Copy .env.example to .env and run prisma db push.
home.heroHeadline: The complete picture of Poland's charging infrastructure, in one place
home.heroSubhead: Normalized, quality-scored data on charging stations and EV models — collected across all 16 provinces, ready to analyze.
home.valueQualityBody: Every station and vehicle record carries a completeness and freshness score — you know what you can rely on.
home.valueNormalizedBody: EIPA/UDT and OpenEV Data are unified into one consistent model — no manual source-format cleanup needed.
home.valueBenchmarkBody: Province coverage, operator share, and connector mix — a reference point for network planning.
home.exploreSubtitle: Five ways to dive into the data — from the vehicle catalog to infrastructure insights.
home.evCatalogDescription: Search brands and models, then open detail pages for battery, range, charging, source, and freshness.
home.stationSearchDescription: Filter stations by location, connector, minimum power, and operator using imported EIPA data.
home.stationMapDescription: View grouped Polish charging stations with province, connector, and minimum-power filters.
home.connectorKnowledgeDescription: Review CCS2, Type 2, CHAdeMO, and unknown connector records with AC/DC context and typical power ranges.
home.chargingInsightsDescription: See top operators, connector distribution, strongest stations, and province coverage from imported station data.
home.b2bCtaTitle: Analytics and reports for charging network operators
home.b2bCtaBody: A benchmark report built on full market coverage, or a featured spot in the operator listing — let's talk about what you need.

contact.title: Get in touch
contact.description: Interested in a custom report or a featured listing among operators? Reach out.
contact.interestReportDescription: A tailored charging infrastructure analysis for your company.
contact.interestFeaturedListingDescription: Highlighting your brand on the operator comparison pages.
contact.interestBothDescription: A custom report and a featured listing.

contact.successBody: We've received your submission and will be in touch soon.

operatorFeatured.heroTitle: Feature your charging network on EVSource
operatorFeatured.heroSubtitle: Starting 290 zł/year. Reach thousands of drivers monthly with a featured listing among charging operators.
operatorFeatured.heroStats: Listed on {stations} charging stations across {operators} operators in {provinces} provinces
operatorFeatured.valuePropsSubtitle: Three things that set our data apart from other sources.
operatorFeatured.visibilityBody: Direct access to EVSource's driver search and location discovery. Your network appears prominently in operator listings.
operatorFeatured.trustBody: Certified, government-sourced data increases credibility. Your infrastructure is backed by official EIPA/UDT Poland records.
operatorFeatured.simpleBody: No integration required—we handle the listing and updates. Just let us know about your network.
operatorFeatured.nextStepsBody: Click the button above to submit your operator information. Our team will review your request and be in touch within 2 business days to discuss pricing, terms, and next steps.

connectors.title: Connector knowledge base
connectors.description: Static reference notes for interpreting connector names in imported charging station and vehicle data. These pages do not claim live compatibility or charger availability.

connectorDetail.incompleteDataBody: Some imported records do not include a connector type that can be classified confidently. This page keeps those records explainable without inventing compatibility, power, or regional support.
connectorDetail.imageCaption: Preview image only: the graphic above shows what this connector type looks like.

connectorDetailError.title: Connector details could not load
connectorDetailError.body: This static connector reference page is temporarily unavailable.

ccs2.description: CCS2 is the main European DC fast charging connector used by most modern EVs and high-power public chargers.

type2.description: Type 2 is the common European AC charging connector for destination charging, home wallboxes, and many public posts.

chademo.description: CHAdeMO is an older DC fast charging connector still found on some imported and legacy EVs.

unknown.description: The imported station record does not identify the connector type clearly enough to classify it.

coverage.title: Infrastructure Coverage
coverage.description: Identify provinces that are underserved or saturated by today's charging infrastructure.
coverage.explainerTitle: What this is, and is not
coverage.explainerBodySuffix: : where charging stations, high-power (150 kW+) chargers, and known-power connectors are concentrated or sparse today, based on imported station data.
coverage.explainerCaveatSuffix: . There is no machine learning model, no route planning, and no population-normalized claims (for example, no "stations per resident" figures) - that would require licensed population data which is out of scope. Provinces with few stations may simply be sparsely populated or rural, not necessarily underserved relative to demand.
coverage.setupRequiredMessage: Coverage analysis is not available yet. Configure the database and run the charging station imports.
coverage.emptyBody: Import charging station data to populate coverage rankings. Stations without a province will be grouped as Unknown province.
coverage.stationsMetricHelper: Charging locations included in the rollup
coverage.hpcStationsMetricHelper: Stations with at least one connector at 150 kW+
coverage.powerAvailabilityMetricHelper: {known} of {total} connectors report a known power
coverage.lowestStationCountSubtitle: Provinces with the fewest charging stations observed.
coverage.lowestHpcCoverageSubtitle: Provinces with the smallest share of stations offering 150 kW+ charging.
coverage.highestStationCountSubtitle: Provinces with the most charging stations observed - the most saturated regions in the current dataset.
coverage.comparisonSubtitle: Rows are ranked by station count, then province name.

coverageError.title: Coverage analysis could not load

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.description: What data evsource.pl collects, why, and how to reach us about it.
privacy.controllerBody: The data controller for personal data processed through evsource.pl is Adateo Rafał Ciesielski, NIP: 7781461342, ul. Polanowska 79, 60-454 Poznań, Poland. For data-protection inquiries, contact kontakt@evsource.pl.
privacy.dataCollectedLeadBody: If you use the lead submission form (for example, to report a station for the report or to inquire about a featured listing), we collect: name (optional), email address, company name (optional), and message (optional). This data is used only to respond to your submission.
privacy.dataCollectedCookieBody: We use a single cookie, NEXT_LOCALE, to remember your chosen interface language (Polish or English). It is set when you change language, stored for 12 months, and is not used for tracking or profiling.
privacy.dataCollectedAnalyticsBody: We use Vercel Analytics and Vercel Speed Insights for anonymous traffic and load-performance measurement. Per the provider's (Vercel's) documentation, these products do not use cookies and do not collect data that identifies a specific individual - they process aggregated visit and performance data. We have not independently verified this beyond Vercel's published documentation.
privacy.legalBasisBody: We process lead-form data based on the controller's legitimate interest (GDPR Art. 6(1)(f)) - responding to inquiries and business contact. If we introduce a newsletter or other marketing communication in the future, it will require separate, explicit consent (GDPR Art. 6(1)(a)).
privacy.retentionBody: We retain lead-submission data until the data subject requests deletion, or until the contact/engagement the submission related to concludes - whichever comes later.
privacy.rightsBody: You have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict processing of, and port your data, and to object to processing based on legitimate interest. You may also lodge a complaint with Poland's data protection authority (UODO).
privacy.contactBody: For questions about personal data, email kontakt@evsource.pl.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.description: The rules for using evsource.pl - kept short and free of boilerplate that doesn't apply.
terms.scopeBody: These terms cover use of the evsource.pl website. The site currently requires no account registration and processes no payments - this covers browsing the data and using the contact/lead forms only.
terms.dataBody: Electric vehicle and charging infrastructure data is sourced from third parties (EIPA / UDT Poland and OpenEV Data) and presented in imported, normalized form. We do not guarantee its completeness, currency, or accuracy. The data is informational and should not be the sole basis for safety-critical decisions (for example, route planning without an energy buffer based solely on a station's status on this site).
terms.noAccountsBody: The site does not currently offer account registration, login, or payment processing. If these features are introduced in the future, these terms will be updated accordingly.
terms.offersBody: Station report submissions, featured listing offers, or other forms of collaboration are not governed by this document - any such arrangement is agreed individually, in separate correspondence or a separate agreement.
terms.liabilityBody: The site is provided "as is," without warranty of uninterrupted operation. We are not liable for damages arising from use of the displayed data for purposes it is not intended for.
terms.contactBody: Questions about these terms can be sent to kontakt@evsource.pl.
